member.name: F:Shapes.RenderState.zTest
member.summary: Current depth buffer compare function. Default is CompareFunction.LessEqual

member.name: F:Shapes.RenderState.zOffsetFactor
member.summary: This ZOffsetFactor scales the maximum Z slope, with respect to X or Y of the polygon, while the other ZOffsetUnits, scales the minimum resolvable depth buffer value. This allows you to force one polygon to be drawn on top of another although they are actually in the same position. For example, if ZOffsetFactor = 0 & ZOffsetUnits = -1, it pulls the polygon closer to the camera, ignoring the polygon’s slope, whereas if ZOffsetFactor = -1 & ZOffsetUnits = -1, it will pull the polygon even closer when looking at a grazing angle.

member.name: F:Shapes.RenderState.zOffsetUnits
member.summary: This ZOffsetUnits value scales the minimum resolvable depth buffer value, while the other ZOffsetFactor scales the maximum Z slope, with respect to X or Y of the polygon. This allows you to force one polygon to be drawn on top of another although they are actually in the same position. For example, if ZOffsetFactor = 0 & ZOffsetUnits = -1, it pulls the polygon closer to the camera, ignoring the polygon’s slope, whereas if ZOffsetFactor = -1 & ZOffsetUnits = -1, it will pull the polygon even closer when looking at a grazing angle.

member.name: F:Shapes.RenderState.colorMask
member.summary: This value will set what channels to render to. By default, it renders to all RGBA channels. This can be useful when you need something to not write to the alpha channel, or when you want to render invisible shapes to the stencil buffer

member.name: F:Shapes.RenderState.stencilComp
member.summary: The stencil buffer function used to compare the reference value to the current contents of the buffer. Default: always

member.name: F:Shapes.RenderState.stencilOpPass
member.summary: What to do with the contents of the stencil buffer if the stencil test (and the depth test) passes. Default: keep

member.name: F:Shapes.RenderState.stencilRefID
member.summary: The stencil buffer id/reference value to be compared against. Default: 0

member.name: F:Shapes.RenderState.stencilReadMask
member.summary: A stencil buffer 8 bit mask as an 0–255 integer, used when comparing the reference value with the contents of the buffer. Default: 255

member.name: F:Shapes.RenderState.stencilWriteMask
member.summary: A stencil buffer 8 bit mask as an 0–255 integer, used when writing to the buffer. Note that, like other write masks, it specifies which bits of stencil buffer will be affected by write (i.e. WriteMask 0 means that no bits are affected and not that 0 will be written). Default: 255

member.summary: Stretches the texture to fit the rectangle, ignoring aspect ratio

member.name: F:Shapes.TextureFillMode.ScaleToFit
member.summary: Scales the texture (preserving aspect ratio) so that it fits within the rectangle

member.name: F:Shapes.TextureFillMode.ScaleAndCropToFill
member.summary: Scales the texture (preserving aspect ratio) so that it covers the rectangle entirely, cropping any overflow

member.summary: Dash style, space & size settings

member.name: F:Shapes.DashStyle.type
member.summary: The type of dash to use

member.name: F:Shapes.DashStyle.space
member.summary: The space in which dashes are defined

member.name: F:Shapes.DashStyle.snap
member.summary: What snapping type to use

member.name: F:Shapes.DashStyle.size
member.summary: Size of dashes in the specified dash space. When using DashSpace.FixedCount, this is the number of dashes

member.name: F:Shapes.DashStyle.spacing
member.summary: Size of spacing between each dash, in the specified dash space. When using DashSpace.FixedCount, this is the dash:space ratio

member.name: F:Shapes.DashStyle.offset
member.summary: An offset of 1 is the size of a whole dash+space period

member.name: F:Shapes.DashStyle.shapeModifier
member.summary: -1 to 1 modifier that allows you to tweak or mirror certain dash types

member.name: P:Shapes.DashStyle.UniformSize
member.summary: Sets both size and spacing to the same value

member.name: M:Shapes.DashStyle.RelativeDashes(Shapes.DashType,System.Single,System.Single,Shapes.DashSnapping,System.Single,System.Single)
member.summary: Creates a dash style where dash lengths are relative to the thickness of the object you're drawing. A dash size of 1 means the dash is as long as the shape is thick
member.param: The type of dash to use (basic, rounded or angled)
The size/length of a single dash. A size of 1 is equal to the thickness of the shape you're using these dashes on
The size/length of the spacing between each dash. A size of 1 is equal to the thickness of the shape you're using these dashes on
What snapping mode to use, if any
Longitudinal offset for the dashes. A value of 1 is the length of a single dash+space period, which means the offset repeats/looks the same at every integer
A modifier for the dash styles, which will set the skewness of angled dashes, for instance. Valid range: -1 to 1

member.name: M:Shapes.DashStyle.FixedDashCount(Shapes.DashType,System.Single,System.Single,Shapes.DashSnapping,System.Single,System.Single)
member.summary: Creates a dash style where you set the number of dashes across the entire shape
member.param: The type of dash to use (basic, rounded or angled)
The fraction of each repeating period that should be space. A value of 0.5f means spaces will have the same size as dashes. Valid range: greater than 0, less than 1
What snapping mode to use, if any
Longitudinal offset for the dashes. A value of 1 is the length of a single dash+space period, which means the offset repeats/looks the same at every integer
A modifier for the dash styles, which will set the skewness of angled dashes, for instance. Valid range: -1 to 1

member.name: M:Shapes.DashStyle.MeterDashes(Shapes.DashType,System.Single,System.Single,Shapes.DashSnapping,System.Single,System.Single)
member.summary: Creates a dash style where dash lengths are specified in meters
member.param: The type of dash to use (basic, rounded or angled)
The size/length of a single dash in meters
The size/length of the spacing between each dash in meters
What snapping mode to use, if any
Longitudinal offset for the dashes. A value of 1 is the length of a single dash+space period, which means the offset repeats/looks the same at every integer
A modifier for the dash styles, which will set the skewness of angled dashes, for instance. Valid range: -1 to 1

member.name: T:Shapes.Draw
member.summary: The primary class in Shapes for all functionality to draw in immediate mode

member.name: P:Shapes.Draw.Matrix
member.summary: The current immediate-mode drawing matrix. Setting this to transform.localToWorldMatrix will make following draw calls be relative to that object

member.name: M:Shapes.Draw.ResetMatrix
member.summary: Resets the matrix to Matrix4x4.identity

member.name: P:Shapes.Draw.MatrixScope
member.summary: using( MatrixScope ){ /*code*/ } lets you modify the matrix within that scope, automatically restoring the previous state once you leave the scope

member.name: M:Shapes.Draw.PushMatrix
member.summary: Pushes the current drawing matrix onto the matrix stack. Calling will restore this state

member.name: M:Shapes.Draw.PopMatrix
member.summary: Restores the drawing matrix to the previously pushed matrix from the stack

member.name: M:Shapes.Draw.ApplyMatrix(UnityEngine.Matrix4x4)
member.summary: Multiplies the drawing matrix by the input matrix. Equivalent to *= matrix;
member.param: The right hand side matrix to multiply by

member.name: P:Shapes.Draw.Position
member.summary: Get or set the origin of the drawing matrix in world space

member.name: P:Shapes.Draw.Position2D
member.summary: Get or set the origin of the drawing matrix X and Y coordinates in world space

member.name: P:Shapes.Draw.Rotation
member.summary: Attempts to get or directly set the rotation of the drawing matrix in world space. Note that, while rare, non-orthogonal matrices cannot be represented with a quaternion rotation, so the getter might not behave as you expect it to. Setting this rotation frequently may be expensive as it will also have to calculate scale in order to retain its per-axis local scale

member.name: P:Shapes.Draw.Angle2D
member.summary: Gets or sets the 2D angle (in radians) that the X axis is pointing in on the world space 2D XY plane. Note that this may have undesired effects if the drawing matrix is not aligned with the Z plane

member.name: P:Shapes.Draw.Right
member.summary: The world space X axis direction of the current drawing matrix (normalized). If you don't need this normalized, or if you know you haven't scaled your matrix, then RightBasis is computationally less expensive

member.name: P:Shapes.Draw.Up
member.summary: The world space Y axis direction of the current drawing matrix (normalized). If you don't need this normalized, or if you know you haven't scaled your matrix, then UpBasis is computationally less expensive

member.name: P:Shapes.Draw.Forward
member.summary: The world space Z axis direction of the current drawing matrix (normalized). If you don't need this normalized, or if you know you haven't scaled your matrix, then ForwardBasis is computationally less expensive

member.name: P:Shapes.Draw.RightBasis
member.summary: The world space X axis basis vector of the current drawing matrix. The magnitude of this vector is the local X scale

member.name: P:Shapes.Draw.UpBasis
member.summary: The world space Y axis basis vector of the current drawing matrix. The magnitude of this vector is the local Y scale

member.name: P:Shapes.Draw.ForwardBasis
member.summary: The world space Z axis basis vector of the current drawing matrix. The magnitude of this vector is the local Z scale

member.name: P:Shapes.Draw.LocalScale
member.summary: Gets or sets the local per-axis scale of the current drawing matrix. This is relatively expensive as it needs to calculate the magnitude of each basis vector

member.name: M:Shapes.Draw.Translate(System.Single,System.Single)
member.summary: Translates (moves) the current drawing matrix by this amount along each of its axes

member.name: M:Shapes.Draw.Translate(System.Single,System.Single,System.Single)
member.summary: Translates (moves) the current drawing matrix by this amount along each of its axes

member.name: M:Shapes.Draw.Translate(UnityEngine.Vector2)
member.summary: Translates (moves) the current drawing matrix by this amount along each of its axes

member.name: M:Shapes.Draw.Translate(UnityEngine.Vector3)
member.summary: Translates (moves) the current drawing matrix by this amount along each of its axes

member.name: M:Shapes.Draw.Rotate(System.Single)
member.summary: Rotates the drawing matrix by this angle around its Z axis
member.param: Angle to rotate by, in radians

member.name: M:Shapes.Draw.Rotate(System.Single,System.Single,System.Single)
member.summary: Rotates the drawing matrix by these angles around each axis, in radians

member.name: M:Shapes.Draw.Rotate(System.Single,UnityEngine.Vector3)
member.summary: Rotates the drawing matrix by this angle (in radians) around the given axis

member.name: M:Shapes.Draw.Rotate(UnityEngine.Quaternion)
member.summary: Rotates the drawing matrix by this quaternion

member.name: M:Shapes.Draw.Scale(System.Single)
member.summary: Scales the drawing matrix uniformly on each axis by this amount

member.name: M:Shapes.Draw.Scale(System.Single,System.Single)
member.summary: Scales the drawing matrix by this amount per axis

member.name: M:Shapes.Draw.Scale(System.Single,System.Single,System.Single)
member.summary: Scales the drawing matrix by this amount per axis

member.name: M:Shapes.Draw.Scale(UnityEngine.Vector2)
member.summary: Scales the drawing matrix by this amount per axis

member.name: M:Shapes.Draw.Scale(UnityEngine.Vector3)
member.summary: Scales the drawing matrix by this amount per axis

member.name: M:Shapes.Draw.SetMatrix(UnityEngine.Matrix4x4)
member.summary: Sets the drawing matrix. Equivalent to directly assigning to Draw.Matrix

member.summary: Sets the drawing matrix to this position, rotation and scale

summary.para: Creates a DrawCommand for drawing in immediate mode.

member.name: M:Shapes.Draw.PrepareForIMGUI
member.summary: Prepares Shapes to draw in IMGUI Repaint events, by initializing the _ScreenParams variable to the current drawing context size. This is necessary as Shapes relies on detecting pixel sizing for anti-aliasing, which isn't set in IMGUI since there's no camera that sets these values for us

member.name: M:Shapes.Draw.Mesh(UnityEngine.Mesh,UnityEngine.Material,UnityEngine.MaterialPropertyBlock)
member.summary: Draws a custom mesh with a custom material as part of a Shapes command buffer. Note: this will ignore global Shapes properties like color, thickness, etc, including render states like blending modes
member.param: The mesh asset to draw. Note: do not destroy this mesh before it has been drawn!
The material asset to draw with. Note: do not destroy this mesh before it has been drawn!
The material property block to apply to the draw call

summary.para: Resets draw style states, including color, but not the drawing matrix

member.name: P:Shapes.Draw.StyleScope
member.summary: using( StyleScope ){ /*code*/ } lets you modify the draw style state within that scope, automatically restoring the previous state once you leave the scope

member.name: M:Shapes.Draw.PushStyle
member.summary: Pushes the current draw style state onto the style stack. Calling will restore this state

member.name: M:Shapes.Draw.PopStyle
member.summary: Restores the draw style state to the previously pushed state from the stack

member.name: P:Shapes.Draw.ColorScope
member.summary: using( ColorScope ){ /*code*/ } lets you modify the color state within that scope, automatically restoring the previously saved color once you leave the scope

member.name: M:Shapes.Draw.PushColor
member.summary: Pushes the current draw color onto the matrix stack. Calling will restore this state

member.name: M:Shapes.Draw.PopColor
member.summary: Restores the draw color state to the previously pushed state from the stack

member.name: M:Shapes.Draw.DashedScope

member.name: M:Shapes.Draw.DashedScope(Shapes.DashStyle)
member.summary: using( DashedScope ){ /*code*/ } temporarily enables and overrides dashes within that scope, automatically restoring the previous state once you leave the scope

member.name: M:Shapes.Draw.GradientFillScope
member.summary: using( GradientFillScope ){ /*code*/ } temporarily enables gradient fill within that scope, automatically restoring the previous state once you leave the scope

member.name: M:Shapes.Draw.GradientFillScope(Shapes.GradientFill)
member.summary: using( GradientFillScope ){ /*code*/ } temporarily enables and overrides gradient fill within that scope, automatically restoring the previous state once you leave the scope

member.summary: Sets the offset of dashes. An offset of 1 is the size of a whole dash+space period, meaning it will repeat at every integer value

member.name: M:Shapes.PolylinePath.ArcTo(UnityEngine.Vector3,Shapes.PolylinePoint,System.Single,System.Single)

member.name: T:Shapes.ShapesBlendMode
member.summary: Various types of blending modes, changing how shapes render on top of the existing colors

member.name: F:Shapes.ShapesBlendMode.Opaque
member.summary: No partial transparency support, but sorts and blends correctly as it writes to the depth buffer, unlike all other blending modes. Note that when MSAA is enabled you get low-resolution fading by means of alpha-to-coverage blending (though this will only work in the game view with MSAA on)

member.name: F:Shapes.ShapesBlendMode.Transparent
member.summary: The classic transparent/alpha-blended mode, where the alpha channel controls opacity. Note that this mode is order dependent, and can't sort correctly when intersecting other transparent shapes

member.name: F:Shapes.ShapesBlendMode.Additive
member.summary: Adds color linearly, good for glowing/brightening effects against dark backgrounds

member.name: F:Shapes.ShapesBlendMode.ColorDodge
member.summary: A harsher non-linear version of additive that goes even brighter when blending with bright colors. Has somewhat unintuitive behaviors when blending with pure black

member.name: F:Shapes.ShapesBlendMode.Screen
member.summary: Softly brightens towards white. Also called "soft additive", this is the opposite operation of multiply, softly blending toward white instead of black Lighten outputs the lightest of the shape and its background

member.name: F:Shapes.ShapesBlendMode.Lighten
member.summary: Outputs the lightest color per-channel of the shape and its background

member.name: F:Shapes.ShapesBlendMode.LinearBurn
member.summary: Darkens linearly. This is the opposite operation of additive. Unlike multiply, linear burn has a tendency to introduce hue shifting

member.name: F:Shapes.ShapesBlendMode.ColorBurn
member.summary: A harsher non-linear version of linear burn that goes even darker when blending with dark colors. Has somewhat unintuitive behaviors when blending with pure white

member.name: F:Shapes.ShapesBlendMode.Multiplicative
member.summary: Is good for tinting/darkening effects against bright backgrounds. It will softly darken toward black, which is the opposite of screen blending which softly brighens to white

member.name: F:Shapes.ShapesBlendMode.Darken
member.summary: Outputs the darkest color per-channel of the shape and its background

member.name: F:Shapes.ShapesBlendMode.Subtractive
member.summary: Subtracts the shape color from the background. It's like linear burn but the colors are inverted and it's annoying to work with

member.name: T:Shapes.TextStyle
member.summary: Text style settings

member.name: F:Shapes.TextStyle.font
member.summary: The TMP font to use when drawing text

member.name: F:Shapes.TextStyle.size
member.summary: The font size to use when drawing text

member.name: F:Shapes.TextStyle.style
member.summary: The font style (normal, bold, italics, etc) to use when drawing text

member.name: F:Shapes.TextStyle.alignment
member.summary: The text alignment to use when drawing text

member.name: F:Shapes.TextStyle.characterSpacing
member.summary: The spacing adjustment between characters to use when drawing text

member.name: F:Shapes.TextStyle.wordSpacing
member.summary: The spacing adjustment between words to use when drawing text

member.name: F:Shapes.TextStyle.lineSpacing
member.summary: The spacing adjustment between lines to use when drawing text

member.name: F:Shapes.TextStyle.paragraphSpacing
member.summary: The spacing adjustment between paragraphs to use when drawing text

member.name: F:Shapes.TextStyle.margins
member.summary: The margins to use when drawing text (in order: left, top, right, bottom)

member.name: F:Shapes.TextStyle.wrap
member.summary: Whether or not to wrap (add line breaks) when the text reaches the end of its containing rectangle

member.name: F:Shapes.TextStyle.overflow
member.summary: How to handle text overflowing the containing rectangle when drawing text

member.name: F:Shapes.TextStyle.curvature
member.summary: The bend curvature of this text, in signed radians per local space meter (aka inverse meters). 0 = no bending. Sign determines bend direction. A positive value will bend the text in a U-shape, a negative value will bend the other direction. If you want to match the radius of a circle, set curvature to the inverse radius curvature = 1/r

member.name: F:Shapes.TextStyle.curvaturePivot
member.summary: The local space position that remains fixed when bending this text (when curvature != 0)
